title: jasminum humile var. siderophyllum
intro: Jasminum humile var. siderophyllum, also known as the silver jasmine, is a climbing plant species belonging to the family Oleaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids, terpenes, and other bioactive compounds. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. In herbal medicine, the leaves and flowers of silver jasmine are commonly prepared as infusions, tinctures, or teas, which can be used to treat various conditions, such as fever, cough, and digestive issues.
